stem: A urban planning study measures the commute time (minutes) for a sample of 15 city workers. The sample mean is 376.1 min with a sample standard deviation of 72.4 min. However, 3 of the 15 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 12 observations have a mean of 362.2 min and standard deviation of 47.1 min. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 376.1, 95% CI = [336.0, 416.2]. Without outliers: mean = 362.2, 95% CI = [335.0, 389.4]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [335.0, 416.2]. Point estimate: 369.1 min.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [335.0, 416.2].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
